title: Manti Turkish Dumplings Yogurt

seo.title: Authentic Manti Turkish Dumplings Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Manti Turkish Dumplings recipe with a flavorful yogurt garlic sauce. This traditional Turkish pasta dish is easier than you think! Get the step-by-step guide.
longTailKeywords: how to make Manti Turkish Dumplings
Manti Turkish Dumplings step by step
best Manti Turkish Dumplings at home
traditional Turkish Manti with yogurt sauce
seo.h1: Authentic Manti Turkish Dumplings with Yogurt Sauce
seo.imageAlt: A plate of authentic Manti Turkish dumplings with a creamy garlic yogurt sauce and a sprinkle of red pepper flakes.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the taste of Turkey with these delicate Manti dumplings, served with a refreshing yogurt sauce – a true culinary delight!

description: Manti Turkish Dumplings are delicate pockets of dough filled with a savory spiced meat mixture, a true jewel of Turkish cuisine. These tiny parcels are traditionally boiled and then served with a generous dollop of creamy garlic yogurt sauce and a drizzle of hot butter infused with red pepper flakes. This iconic dish offers a delightful blend of textures and flavors, making it a comforting and satisfying meal for any occasion.

instructions.title: Prepare the Dough
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine flour, 1 teaspoon salt, egg, and warm water. Mix until a shaggy dough forms, then knead on a lightly floured surface for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Cover with a damp cloth and let rest for 30 minutes.

instructions.title: Make the Filling
instructions.text: While the dough rests, prepare the filling. In a medium bowl, combine ground meat, finely minced onion, 1/2 teaspoon salt, black pepper, and 1/2 teaspoon red pepper flakes if using. Mix well until all ingredients are evenly distributed.

instructions.title: Roll and Cut the Dough
instructions.text: Divide the rested dough into two equal portions. On a well-floured surface, roll out one portion very thinly, about 1-2mm thick, into a large rectangle. Using a knife or pastry wheel, cut the dough into small squares, approximately 1.5 cm by 1.5 cm.

instructions.title: Fill and Shape the Manti
instructions.text: Place a tiny amount of the meat filling (about 1/4 teaspoon) in the center of each dough square. Bring all four corners of the square together in the center and pinch them firmly to seal, creating a small boat-like or pouch shape. Ensure the filling is completely enclosed. Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.

instructions.title: Cook the Manti
instructions.text: Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Carefully drop the manti into the boiling water in batches to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 8-10 minutes, or until the manti float to the surface and the dough is tender. Remove with a slotted spoon.

instructions.title: Prepare the Yogurt and Butter Sauces
instructions.text: While manti cook, prepare the sauces. For the yogurt sauce, whisk together plain yogurt, minced garlic, and 1/4 teaspoon salt in a bowl. For the butter sauce, melt butter in a small pan over medium heat. Add 1 teaspoon red pepper flakes and dried mint if using, and cook for 30 seconds until fragrant.

instructions.title: Assemble and Serve
instructions.text: Arrange the cooked manti on a serving plate. Spoon a generous amount of the garlic yogurt sauce over the top. Drizzle with the hot spiced butter sauce. Serve immediately, perhaps with an extra sprinkle of dried mint or sumac for garnish.

tips: For truly authentic manti, aim for small, consistent dumpling sizes; this allows them to cook evenly and provides a delicate texture.
Do not overwork the dough; knead it just until smooth and elastic to ensure tender manti that are not tough.
Serve Manti immediately after assembling, as the hot dumplings best absorb the flavors of the cool garlic yogurt and warm spiced butter.
article:
A culinary journey to Turkey is incomplete without savoring Manti, the country's beloved tiny dumplings, often described as Turkish pasta or noodles. These delicate pockets of flavor, typically filled with spiced ground meat and served with a generous dollop of garlicky yogurt and a drizzle of vibrant chili-infused butter, represent the heart of Turkish home cooking. Far more than just a dish, Manti is an experience – a labor of love often shared among families, a testament to culinary heritage, and an absolute delight for the taste buds. If you're ready to dive into the rich world of Turkish cuisine, mastering the classic Manti Turkish Dumplings recipe is a truly rewarding endeavor.

## What are Manti Turkish Dumplings?

Manti are small, intricately folded dumplings, a staple across Turkey and various Central Asian, Caucasian, and Balkan cuisines. While variations exist, Turkish Manti are particularly famous for their diminutive size, especially those from Kayseri, where legend dictates that 40 Manti should fit onto a single spoon. The traditional filling consists of finely ground beef or lamb, seasoned with onion, salt, and black pepper. The magic, however, truly happens when these boiled or steamed dumplings are served. They arrive swimming in a cool, tangy garlic yogurt sauce, often accompanied by a sizzling butter sauce infused with dried mint and Aleppo pepper (pul biber), sometimes with a sprinkle of sumac for an extra layer of tartness. It's a symphony of textures and temperatures – warm, tender pasta, rich meat, cool creamy yogurt, and spicy, aromatic butter – that makes Manti an unforgettable dish.

## Ingredients for Manti

To embark on your Manti Turkish Dumplings recipe adventure, gather these essential components:

For the Dough:
*   2 cups (240g) all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
*   1 large egg
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1/2 cup (120ml) water (or more, as needed)

For the Filling:
*   1 lb (450g) ground beef or lamb (at least 80/20 fat ratio for flavor)
*   1 small onion, very finely grated or minced
*   1/2 teaspoon salt
*   1/4 teaspoon black pepper
*   Optional: a pinch of red pepper flakes

For the Garlic Yogurt Sauce:
*   2 cups (480g) plain full-fat yogurt (preferably Turkish yogurt or Greek yogurt)
*   2-3 cloves garlic, minced
*   1/2 teaspoon salt

For the Chili Butter Sauce:
*   3-4 tablespoons unsalted butter
*   1-2 tablespoons dried mint
*   1-2 teaspoons Aleppo pepper (pul biber) or paprika
*   Optional: a pinch of sumac for garnish

## Step-by-Step Manti Recipe Instructions

Creating Manti is a labor of love, but following this Manti Turkish Dumplings recipe step-by-step will guide you to success.

1.  **Prepare the Dough:** In a large bowl, combine flour, egg, and salt. Gradually add water, mixing until a shaggy dough forms. Turn it out onto a floured surface and knead for 8-10 minutes until smooth and elastic. It should be firm, not sticky. Cover with plastic wrap and let it rest for at least 30 minutes at room temperature. This resting period is crucial for easy rolling.
2.  **Make the Filling:** While the dough rests, combine ground meat, grated onion, salt, and black pepper in a bowl. Mix well with your hands until thoroughly combined. Do not overmix, as it can make the meat tough.
3.  **Roll and Cut the Dough:** Divide the rested dough into 2-3 equal portions. On a generously floured surface, roll out one portion very thinly, about 1/16 to 1/8 inch thick (2-3 mm). The thinner, the better for authentic Manti. Using a sharp knife or a pastry wheel, cut the dough into small squares, approximately 1x1 inch (2.5x2.5 cm).
4.  **Fill and Shape the Manti:** Place a tiny amount (about 1/4 teaspoon) of the meat filling in the center of each dough square. There are several ways to fold Manti, but the most common is to bring all four corners to the center and pinch them together tightly, creating a small "pouch" or boat-like shape. Alternatively, fold two opposite corners to meet, then fold the other two over to meet in the middle, creating a tiny parcel. Ensure they are sealed well to prevent the filling from escaping during cooking. Place the shaped Manti on a floured baking sheet, making sure they don't touch. Repeat with the remaining dough and filling.
5.  **Cook the Manti:** Bring a large pot of salted water to a rolling boil. Carefully drop the Manti into the boiling water in batches, making sure not to overcrowd the pot. Cook for 8-10 minutes, or until the Manti float to the surface and the dough is tender. Remove with a slotted spoon and drain well.
6.  **Prepare the Sauces:** While the Manti cook, prepare the garlic yogurt. In a bowl, whisk together the plain yogurt, minced garlic, and salt until smooth. For the chili butter, melt the butter in a small saucepan over medium heat. Add the dried mint and Aleppo pepper, stirring for about 30 seconds until fragrant and the butter turns a vibrant red. Be careful not to burn the spices.

## Tips for Perfect Turkish Manti

*   **Dough Consistency is Key:** The dough for Manti should be firm and elastic. If it's too soft, it will be hard to roll thinly and will stick. If it's too dry, it will crack. Adjust water or flour as needed during kneading.
*   **Roll Thin, Cut Small:** The hallmark of authentic Manti, especially Kayseri Manti, is their small size and thin dough. This ensures a delicate texture and a pleasant meat-to-dough ratio.
*   **Seal Tightly:** Take your time to pinch the Manti edges securely. A well-sealed Manti prevents the filling from escaping and keeps the dumplings intact.
*   **Don't Overcook:** Manti cooks relatively quickly. Overcooked Manti can become mushy. Test one after 8 minutes to check for doneness.
*   **Generous Flour Dusting:** Use plenty of flour on your work surface and baking sheets to prevent the dough and shaped Manti from sticking.
*   **High-Quality Yogurt:** The creamy, tangy yogurt sauce is just as important as the dumplings themselves. Use full-fat, plain yogurt for the best flavor and texture.

## Serving Suggestions for Manti

Once your Manti are cooked and drained, it's time to assemble this magnificent dish. Arrange the warm Manti on individual serving plates or a large platter. Spoon a generous amount of the cold garlic yogurt sauce over the top, ensuring every Manti is coated. Finally, drizzle the hot, aromatic chili butter sauce over the yogurt. Garnish with an extra sprinkle of dried mint, Aleppo pepper, or sumac for color and flavor. Manti is typically served as a main course and is hearty enough on its own. The contrast of hot Manti, cool yogurt, and warm, spicy butter creates an explosion of flavors and textures that is truly unique.

## Manti Variations and Substitutions

While the traditional Manti Turkish Dumplings recipe with ground meat and yogurt sauce is classic, there are regional variations and ways to adapt the dish:

*   **Regional Styles:** Beyond Kayseri Manti (tiny, pinched at the top), you might encounter Boşnak Manti (larger, often baked after boiling) or Sivas Manti (also larger, sometimes shaped differently). Some regions bake the Manti before serving with yogurt, giving them a slightly crispy texture.
*   **Filling Alternatives:** For those who don't eat meat, Manti can be made with a lentil filling (puy lentils, onion, spices) or a potato filling (mashed potato, herbs, spices). Chicken mince is also a popular alternative to red meat.
*   **Sauce Innovations:** While garlic yogurt and chili butter are standard, some prefer a simple tomato sauce base, especially if they are lactose intolerant. A sprinkle of fresh dill or parsley can also be a lovely addition to the yogurt sauce. Some Manti recipes call for a combination of tomato paste and butter, cooked down before adding chili and mint.
*   **Dried Manti:** For longer storage, Manti can be dried after shaping. This is often done in villages and then stored for months, ready to be boiled when needed.

Manti is more than just Turkish pasta and noodles; it's a culinary masterpiece that brings people together. Preparing this Manti Turkish Dumplings recipe is a wonderful way to connect with Turkish culture and create a truly satisfying and delicious meal. Don't be intimidated by the small size; the effort is well worth the incredible flavor payoff.
